idea-plugin.name: Zinki for Claude
idea-plugin.description:
Zinki for Claude — AI Code Skills for Go Developers

Run Claude Code AI skills on selected Go code directly from GoLand's built-in terminal. Seven skills, zero context switching.

Skills

  • Explain Code (Ctrl+Alt+E) — Step-by-step with ASCII diagrams, analogies, gotchas
  • Review Code (Ctrl+Alt+R) — Security, performance, concurrency, readability
  • Simplify Code (Ctrl+Alt+S) — Go idioms, runs unit tests to verify
  • Write Docs (Ctrl+Alt+D) — godoc comments, markdown docs, or docstrings
  • Generate Tests (Ctrl+Alt+T) — Table-driven Go tests with edge cases
  • Fix Bug (Ctrl+Alt+B) — Diagnose, fix, and verify with go test
  • Refactor (Ctrl+Alt+F) — Idiomatic Go restructuring

Prerequisites

  • Claude Code CLI: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
  • Authenticate once: run claude in a terminal and log in
  • Go installed and on PATH
  • PowerShell available (Windows) or bash (macOS/Linux)

Usage

  1. Select Go code in the editor
  2. Right-click → Zinki for Claude → choose a skill
  3. Set options in the dialog
  4. Claude runs in the built-in terminal — output streams directly
  5. Click Save as MD in the notification to save output as Markdown
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.1.0
  • Added: Write Docs, Generate Tests, Fix Bug, Refactor skills
  • Added: Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Alt+E/R/S/D/T/B/F)
  • Added: Plugin icon in context menu and install dialog
  • Added: Save output as Markdown notification
  • Added: Auto-include file name and package context in prompts
1.0.0
  • Initial release: Explain, Review, Simplify Code

action.description: Explain selected Go code using Claude
Review selected Go code using Claude
Simplify selected Go code using Claude
Generate documentation for selected Go code using Claude
Generate Go tests for selected code using Claude
Diagnose and fix a bug in selected Go code using Claude
Refactor selected Go code using Claude
